TextENGNew: "......Mounds of corpses, everywhere."
"It's a terrible sight......"
The miserable remains of those who appeared to have been beaten by rioters lay here and there along the roadside.
Even Hanyuu shook her head slightly at the overpowering stench of death and the swarms of flies that gathered......
"......But it's grown quiet."
"Yes, it has......"
"By now, those who let the demon of paranoia into their hearts have mostly killed each other off and died out."
That groaning wind, like the low rumble of some ominous dragon...... had at some point fallen silent.
In its place, all that enveloped Hinamizawa was the silence of death.
The screams of those who had surrendered their hearts to the demon and given themselves over to slaughter could no longer be heard......
"But it never hurts to be cautious."
"True. I can't die before I see how this mad world ends."
"Where are you going?"
"The basement of the Irie Clinic. Don't you think that's the safest place in Hinamizawa right now?"
The underground research ward was already wrapped in the silence of death.
As long as she didn't mind the corpses and the trails of bright red splatter staining the white walls, it would be the perfect place to hole up for a while.
"......Are there still some violent ones left in the village?"
"Yes...... I think it should calm down soon, but for now......"
"Then it's decided. Let's wait for the heat to die down. There should be someone's drinks and snacks down there, and the bathrooms are clean."
Rika and Hanyuu made their way toward the Irie Clinic.
To that den of incompetents who had spent all that time in research and still managed to do nothing at all......
They passed through animal trails in the mountains and along the footpaths between the rice paddies.
Sometimes they held their breath, listening for signs of anyone nearby; other times they ran like rabbits.
Then, in one motion, they vaulted the guardrail — and there was the town of Okinomiya.
From somewhere in the distance, the wind occasionally carried shouts and the smell of something burning.
The town was deathly still, but it was not dead.
People were simply keeping out of sight from the rioters who had let paranoia into their hearts and surrendered to rage.
"......It might be more dangerous here than in Hinamizawa."
"I'd have to agree...... Everything about this place gives me a bad feeling......"
"There are riots here too, then — from the virus panic......?"
"This is just my theory, but — I'm starting to think this might actually be a symptom of the virus itself."
"That might be right."
"What do you mean......?"
``So the virus is doing something to people's brains......?``
changeReason: Old translation adds 'human version of rabies' comparison not in the Japanese. New translation stays closer to source: 'the virus is doing something to people's brains'.

TextENGNew: "I thought everyone was just going mad from fear of a killer virus...... but that alone doesn't explain everything."
"A sickness that drives people to irrational fear and suspicion, until those feelings consume them completely......"
"Does a disease like that really exist? I mean, they never said anything about that on TV......"
"TV doesn't always tell the truth. And besides — the killer virus part might have been a misunderstanding entirely."
"......Either way, it's a terrifying virus — that much is certain."
"But then why are we all right?"
"......Maybe we have some kind of resistance?"
"Well, even if we're showing no symptoms, I can't rule out the possibility that we're still carrying it."
"Is it really all right, then? For us to walk into the city like this?"
"Shouldn't be a problem. Not looking at things the way they are."
We advanced carefully, checking that the streets were empty as we went.
The roads were lined with abandoned cars, left in long rows.
At various intersections, cars that had crashed into each other still smoldered.
On the sidewalks too, we could see the scattered remains of people who had no doubt been killed on suspicion of being infected......
"I just hope Shion and Mion's parents are still keeping their heads......"
"......I just hope Dad and Mom are holding everyone together."
"Shion-san does have a tendency to let her temper get away from her......"
"Shii-chan's the type where there's a big difference between when she's calm and when she's not...... I'm a little worried too."
"It'll be fine. When it counts, Mom is someone Shion can't argue with. ......I'm sure Mom is holding everyone together."
If Oryou-san had still been in good health, the village council of Hinamizawa would never have lost its composure either.
Mion already sensed that strong leadership could prevent chaos before it began.
"I think what this virus does is drive people from anxiety and fear into aggression. ......That's why I think strong leadership, solidarity, and trust are what let people keep their heads."
"So the fear around the virus amplified things...... and sent that fear into a runaway spiral?"
"That makes sense. At least while the Self-Defense Force was here and doing their job, nothing strange happened in Hinamizawa either."
"That's right. ......After the SDF left, everyone got anxious all at once, and then they started going strange one by one......"
Satoko spoke up as though suddenly remembering something.
"Come to think of it, Rika said something like that too! ......She said that people who had the strength of heart not to surrender to the demon would be all right......"
"Rika-chan said that?"
"She did. ......I'd taken it to mean something like mind over matter, but......"
changeReason: Different idiom: old uses 'illness starts in the mind' (病は気から) while new uses 'mind over matter'. These convey different nuances of the Japanese proverb.

TextENGNew: "Perhaps...... she meant it literally......"
"......When Rena and I worked ourselves into a fury trying to save Satoko. ......Maybe we were dangerously close back then too."
"Maybe so. ......But even when our feelings blazed hottest, we kept our heads."
"Hot heart, cool mind. ......Maybe the reason we didn't end up like the others is that Mion's club drilled it into us."

"When you lose your cool and surrender your heart to passion, you go off the rails. ......If that's what they called the demon, then the legend of Oni-ga-fuchi Village that's been passed down since ancient times — that might be what it refers to."
"Something about demons rising from the swamp, wasn't it?"
"I'm sure the same thing happened back then, long ago. ......And the people who were consumed by paranoia and ran amok must have been called demons."
"......I see. I feel like I'm starting to get the picture."
"But the fact that the legend survived means the crisis passed and people lived through it. ......That means this isn't hopeless. It means there's hope."
"Right. This commotion isn't going to destroy the world. It means there's definitely an end to it."
"Is there nothing we can do but wait for Oyashiro-sama to descend......?"
"According to the legend, that's how it went. ......There was nothing the people could do — only wait for Oyashiro-sama's descent."
"......Even with things this bad, all we can do is wait......?"
"If the legend holds, yes."
"God, really! Oyashiro-sama! If you're somewhere watching this, please come down and put an end to it!!"
"Hepshoo!"
"Even a deity catches colds?"
"Auau. Sneezing is not humanity's exclusive privilege, you know."
Inside the Irie Clinic, the rioters had ransacked everything down to the last corner.
A mob that believed a rumor claiming a cure was hidden here had descended upon it en masse.
That, however, was the aboveground portion. It had not reached the secret underground research ward.
"There's nothing here. ......If there had been, Irie would have acted long ago."
"......I can't bring myself to look......"
changeReason: Opposite meaning: old says 'We mustn't avert our eyes' (duty to look) while new says 'I can't bring myself to look' (inability to look). Japanese 目を背けずにはいられない means 'cannot help but look away' — new translation is correct.

TextENGNew: Within the ruined clinic, any number of corpses lay scattered.
The people who had convinced themselves a cure was here must have, in the end, convinced themselves that someone was hoarding it — and killed each other for it......
Hanyuu covered her eyes, but Rika pressed onward without hesitation.
Among the dead were faces she recognized, but Rika showed no sign of being moved.
They passed through the hidden door and descended underground.
Beyond the security-locked classified door, it was safer than anywhere above ground.
After all, every last researcher had either killed each other or taken their own lives. There is no safety greater than complete vacancy......
The security system confirmed that it was Furude Rika, and the door opened.
Watching it close again behind her, even Rika — who had not flinched at the sight of the corpses — felt some small measure of relief.
changeReason: New translation references different characters/places: Rika.

TextENGNew: ``Now we can sleep, or help ourselves to whoever's stash of snacks — whatever we like. There's no one left to object.``
changeReason: New translation provides substantially different content with different details and meaning.

TextENGNew: "To stay this composed amid such devastation...... you are either very resilient, or very thick-skinned......"
"I've lost count of how many deaths I've lived through — my own included, more times than I have toes to count on. Do you really think some stranger's corpse is going to rattle me at this point?"
"......Hmm. The Rika of long ago was a little more timid and age-appropriate, I must say......"
"What exactly is there left for me to be frightened of? ......Fine, then — bring me a ghost. I might manage to look a little surprised at that."
"Wh-what is it, Rika? N-no frightening each other, please......"
In the supposedly deserted underground ward...... she thought she had heard something just now.
Her imagination......?
The two exchanged a glance, then held their breath and strained their ears......
"......You hear it...... don't you......?"
"A-a ghost?!"
"......You're half a ghost yourself — what are you scared of? But what is that sound......?"
From somewhere deep within, a dull thudding — thud...... thud...... — repeated over and over.
The two exchanged a nod and moved in the direction of the sound, straining their ears......
"'Kill 'em, kill 'em!! Kill the infected scum!! Kill 'em and burn the bodies!!"
A group of perhaps ten rioters — young adults — was rushing down the main road.
Their clothes were soaked in blood, and their eyes were a wild, bloodshot crimson.
The bats and iron pipes in their hands dripped red, twisted into grotesque shapes.
"If those people found us...... it would be really, really bad......"
"College students, maybe. ......Not quite the same as the elderly of Hinamizawa."
"We're all right. They haven't spotted us."
The deranged young men would fly into motion at the slightest sound and charge off in pursuit.
Anyone who fled was hunted down without mercy,
anyone who begged for their life was attacked all the same, and sometimes, when someone fought back, it was the rioters themselves who lost their lives.
changeReason: Different framing: old says 'Those who begged for their lives, and occasionally those who fought' while new says 'anyone who begged was attacked, and sometimes the attackers themselves fell'. Changes who dies.

TextENGNew: It was the work of true demons, drunk on blood.
......This sight was, in all likelihood, the very massacre the legend of Oni-ga-fuchi Village described.
"Is the office far?"
"......The distance isn't that far, but if we're avoiding the main road, it's surprisingly tricky......"
"Every main road is either crawling with lunatics running amok or becoming a battleground — one or the other."
"Safety first. ......?! Quiet!"
From somewhere behind them, the clatter of an empty can rolling came to their ears.
Someone had kicked over one of the can-trap alarms Satoko had rigged.
Before they could even hold their breath, the threatening shouts of rioters could be heard approaching.
Hunting for someone to kill, they were scouring every back alley with relentless thoroughness.
"We're going to be caught up to......!"
"But we can't run! They'll hear us! We have to hide and wait them out!"
"Pull those trash cans over to make a hiding place!! Hurry!!"
"Kei-chan, bring that one over here!!"
"Y-yeah......!! ......Watch out!"
A fatal blunder. Keiichi's foot slipped on something strewn around the trash can, and he went down, taking the can with him.
A tremendous clatter rang out through the alley......
``Oi!! Something's there!! Infected scum, for sure!!``
changeReason: Different tone: 'another infected' (neutral) vs 'infected scum' (hateful). The Japanese 感染野郎 includes the derogatory 野郎.

TextENGNew: In response, a chorus of howls — indistinguishable between ecstasy and fury — thundered in from multiple directions.
Those howls came not only from behind but from ahead as well.
``We're caught between them!!``
changeReason: Military term 'pincer attack' replaced with plain 'caught between them'. Different register and specificity.

TextENGNew: "We can't fight! We'll just keep drawing more enemies!!"
"We push straight through! Sprint for the office!"
"Ow! Ow ow ow ow?! S-seriously...?!"
"Keiichi-kun?! Did you twist it?!"
Everyone's blood ran cold at once. Their best — and only — option had just been stripped away.
Mion, lending Keiichi her shoulder, snapped orders in rapid succession.
"Satoko! Find a retreat route!! Rena, grab a weapon!!"
"Already on it!!"
"This is all I could find...!"
Rena scooped up a no-parking sign. Thrown at someone, it might be enough to make them flinch.
But the greatest weapon of all was the look in Rena's eyes — the resolve of someone who had steeled herself to protect the people she loved.
When Rena had made up her mind to protect what mattered to her, there was not a shred of hesitation or mercy in her.
Satoko, with the brilliant situational awareness she brought to setting traps, calculated the scenario in an instant.
If the way forward and back were both cut off, the only direction left was up.
Use the trash can as a step, climb up to that eave, and from there......
But no — impossible! Keiichi had twisted his ankle. Without a ladder, there was absolutely no way!!
changeReason: Different body part: 'sprained his leg' vs 'twisted his ankle'. Japanese 足を挫いて says foot/ankle (足), not leg.

TextENGNew: "I-I'm fine! It's just temporary, something like this is nothing......!"
It was true that he wasn't completely unable to walk. A little rest and he'd probably recover quickly enough.
But even so, counting on him to see them through what was coming now was far too uncertain......!
"Why would you twist your ankle at a time like this!! You really are such an idiot, Keiichi-san!! Ugh!! If only there were a ladder......!!"
"If it's just me, I can manage somehow! Don't worry about me — get up there!!"
"If you have time to talk nonsense, spend it praying for a ladder to appear!!"
"Oh heavens above, Oyashiro-sama!! Please, please grant us a ladder!!"
"Ha! If a prayer saves us, I'll dance with my belly button showing!"
"M-Mii-chan...!! Th-the ladder...!!"
"Huh?!"
From above the eave, an actual ladder was being lowered down.
A girl could be seen trying, with unsteady hands, to offer a wooden ladder out to them from above.
changeReason: Different material: old says 'stainless steel ladder' while new says 'wooden ladder'. Japanese 木製の梯子 explicitly says wooden (木製).

TextENGNew: What was easily light for most would be more than enough weight for such a small girl. She might lose her balance and fall at any moment.
"Thank you!! You can let go now, I've got it!"
Rena took the ladder and propped it up quickly.
"Kei-chan, can you climb?!"
"I have to climb — otherwise those people are going to get themselves killed by Rena."
"Thanks. If you can climb, then we might get through this without any pointless casualties."
Rena had snapped the wooden frame of the sign and sharpened it into a makeshift spear.
The thought of throwing the sign to make someone flinch didn't even register as an option for her. It was both terrifying and reassuring.
Even so, it was no substitute for the ladder.
"Thank you!! I don't know who you are, but I'm so grateful!!"
"......It was the least I could do, and more."
"It's fine, I can climb......"
"Sorry, but hurry! They're here!!"
"The infected scum are right here!! Bunch of 'em!!"
"Kill 'em, kill 'em!! HyahahahahahahaHA!!"
"Enough! If you're ready to kill, you'd better be ready to be killed too!"
"Rena-san, get up!!"
Once Rena had climbed the ladder, Mion immediately hauled it back up.
The girl indicated with a gesture that they could leave the ladder there, then went from the eave into a second-floor window.
Below, the mob of young rioters hurled abuse while searching for a way to climb up to the eave.
"This way. ......This place isn't safe forever, either."
"Thank you so much! You've saved our lives!"
"We're going back out another window?"
"......Those people will absolutely find a way to climb up."
They climbed back out through a different window, used an air conditioner unit as a stepping stone, and entered the neighboring building through its window.
It appeared she had been moving along the second-floor level of the shopping arcade, avoiding the dangerous ground below.
Moving from building to building, rooftop to rooftop, they eventually felt certain they had escaped to safety......
They arrived at the second floor of what appeared to be a combined home and shop.
This seemed to be her shelter.
"......We're safe. Looks like we've shaken them completely."
"They just went looking for other prey. ......Those people simply want to kill. That's all."
"The street below looks like a sea full of sharks......"
"......Thank you, truly. You saved us."
"Whether we're actually saved...... no one can say yet."
"We've only escaped the immediate danger. ......But still — thank you."
``I suppose... it is something worth being thanked for.``
changeReason: Old says 'I did earn your thanks' (accepting credit); new says 'it is something worth being thanked for' (cautious acknowledgment).

TextENGNew: "Mmhm. ......It really was."
There was no reason for anyone to risk their life for a stranger.
In Okinomiya as it stood now, if you could manage to be shut inside with your doors locked tight, the best thing was to cover your ears and huddle there — that much was clear.
"I'm Maebara Keiichi. And you?"
"......People call me Une."
"I'm Rena. That's Mii-chan and Satoko-chan."
"Pleased to meet you. You helped us at precisely the right moment."
"It was fortunate — but luck doesn't always hold."
"Well, luck is part of the skill."
"Is it just you here, Une-chan? Are there others?"
"There were. ......But they haven't come back."
If they had stayed hidden here, they would have been safe.
But it is a sad truth of human nature — once safety is secured, one immediately wants to risk it again......
Checking on family, or simply trying to get home. Once you have safety, somehow you can never quite stay put......
The place was evidently well established as a shelter.
About a dozen bottles of drinking water were lined up, and four grocery bags stuffed with snacks lay scattered around.
However many people had been here, as long as they didn't demand luxury, they could have held out for three days.
"When you've built a safe place like this...... you want to bring your family and friends there."
"Right. Staying here with your ears covered is the correct choice. ......But thanks to Une-chan, we were saved."
"The words 'lifesaver' simply aren't enough to express our gratitude......"
"Are there still a lot of those dangerous people out there?"
"There are hardly none of them — which is to say, there are quite a lot."
"That's fair. But they can't be running around all through the night either."
"The neighborhood watch in Hinamizawa kept sentries posted through the night too."
"They are frightened themselves, after all. ......I can't imagine them snoring away with their guard down."
"Even so, it should be a little safer than broad daylight. The Sonozaki Group office isn't far, is it?"
"......Well, as the crow flies, no."
"We should wait and see for a while. There's no need to rush."
"Right. Une-chan — would you mind telling us a little about the state of the town?"
"......It's not a long story, necessarily. ..........."
